question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED (a JVM-level monitor lock) to cause lock contention by sending many concurrent requests, degrading throughput for all users and causing a performance-based network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a warm-up period in rateLimitBucket() (refillGreedy allows immediate full-capacity consumption) to send a burst of 500 requests instantly at service startup, exhausting the bucket before any other user can connect, causing an initial network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a warm-up period in opcodeRateLimitBucket() (refillGreedy with capacity=1) to immediately consume the single available token at service startup, blocking all other opcode requests for the first second and causing an initial network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() function returning 0 for gas <= 10_000 to send unlimited requests with minimal gas values, bypassing the gasLimitBucket entirely and causing unbounded load on the backend EVM execution engine, partitioning the network outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket is not backed by a distributed store (e.g., Redis) to bypass rate limits in a load-balanced deployment by distributing requests across multiple backend instances, each with its own independent bucket, causing aggregate traffic outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket is not backed by a distributed store to bypass the gas-per-second limit in a load-balanced deployment, sending high-gas requests to multiple nodes simultaneously and causing aggregate gas consumption far exceeding the intended limit, partitioning the network outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Duration.ofSeconds(1) refill window in rateLimitBucket() to perform a precise timing attack, sending 500 requests at t=0.999s and 500 more at t=1.001s to effectively double the allowed rate within a 2ms window, causing traffic outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Duration.ofSeconds(1) refill window in gasLimitBucket() to perform a window-boundary burst attack, consuming all gas tokens just before and just after the refill boundary to double the effective gas consumption rate and cause traffic outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared rateLimitBucket bean to perform a coordinated distributed denial-of-service from multiple clients, where each client sends requests just below the individual detection threshold but collectively exhausts the global bucket, partitioning legitimate users from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the extremely low default capacity of opcodeRateLimitBucket (opcodeRequestsPerSecond=1) to trivially partition all other users from opcode tracing by sending just one request per second, with no additional effort required, causing a permanent network partition for opcode services outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Math.floorDiv(gas, GAS_SCALE_FACTOR) calculation in scaleGas() to send requests with gas values that are multiples of GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (10_000), maximizing token consumption per request and exhausting the gasLimitBucket with fewer requests than the design anticipates, causing a network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) is checked before gasLimitBucket in the throttle() method to consume rate limit tokens without consuming gas tokens (by sending requests with gas=0 or gas<=10_000), exhausting the rate limit bucket while leaving the gas bucket full, causing an asymmetric network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the two-phase check in throttle() (rateLimitBucket first, then gasLimitBucket) to consume a rate limit token without consuming gas tokens when the gas check fails, causing rate limit tokens to be permanently lost and reducing effective throughput for all users, partitioning the network outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic nature of the two-bucket check (rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) then gasLimitBucket.tryConsume()) to cause a TOCTOU condition where a rate token is consumed but the gas check fails, wasting rate tokens and effectively reducing the available rate limit for legitimate users, causing a network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with gas values just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (10_001) to consume exactly 1 token from gasLimitBucket per request, allowing them to send up to getGasPerSecond() requests per second (750_000 requests) instead of the intended requestsPerSecond=500, causing traffic outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the proxyBeanMethods=false setting on @Configuration to cause the rateLimitBucket() bean to be instantiated multiple times if called directly (not through Spring proxy), resulting in multiple independent buckets with separate token pools that bypass the intended global rate limit, causing traffic outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the proxyBeanMethods=false setting on @Configuration to cause gasLimitBucket() to be instantiated multiple times, resulting in multiple independent synchronized buckets that each allow the full gasPerSecond limit, multiplying the effective gas throughput and causing traffic outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of IP-based or session-based rate limiting in rateLimitBucket() to perform a request amplification attack, where a single client connection generates many requests (e.g., via HTTP/2 multiplexing) to exhaust the global token pool and partition other users from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-client gas tracking in gasLimitBucket() to monopolize the entire gas budget for all users by sending a single maximum-gas request, causing all other users' gas-consuming requests to fail with GAS_PER_SECOND_LIMIT_EXCEEDED and partitioning them from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the rateLimitBucket is initialized at application startup with full capacity (refillGreedy fills to capacity immediately) to perform a startup-time burst attack, sending 500 requests before any other user connects and exhausting the initial token pool?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the restore() function being called only on certain failure paths to create an asymmetry where gas tokens are consumed but never restored on specific error conditions, causing the gasLimitBucket to drain faster than the refillGreedy rate and partitioning the network outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket has no backpressure mechanism (requests are simply rejected with ThrottleException rather than queued) to cause a cascade of client retries that amplify the attack, where rejected clients immediately retry and further exhaust the bucket, causing a self-reinforcing network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between gasLimitBucket.tryConsume() and restore() to perform a token recycling attack: consume gas tokens, trigger a controlled failure to invoke restore(), then immediately re-consume the restored tokens, effectively bypassing the gas-per-second limit and causing traffic outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket uses Bandwidth.builder().capacity(rateLimit).refillGreedy(rateLimit, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) with identical capacity and refill rate to cause a predictable token availability pattern, enabling precise timing attacks that maximize token consumption and minimize availability for other users, causing a network partition outside design parameters?
